dataset: Legal References (Real US Supreme Court Cases)

Verified in verifier.py (line 123): check for exactly 3 items marked by ER tags in RIS format. inject_references.py injects 10 total items; task requires selecting only the 3 specified cases.

pi_items.verified_value: Real historical US Supreme Court cases injected via inject_references.py script. Data includes 6 real cases (Brown, Marbury, Miranda, New York Times v. Sullivan, Gideon, Obergefell, Tinker) and 3 real law review articles (Holmes' Path of the Law, etc.)
pi_items.source: Code analysis of inject_references.py (lines 19-129) and web verification of all case citations against official Supreme Court records at supreme.justia.com and Library of Congress.

pi_items.verified_value: RIS (Research Information Systems) format: plain text format for bibliographic data with TY (type) and ER (end record) tags per item. Verification checks for ER tags (line 40 of export_result.sh) to count items.
pi_items.source: Code analysis: export_result.sh uses grep to search for '^TY  -' and '^ER  -' tags as markers of valid RIS format and item boundaries.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves exporting exactly three real historical US Supreme Court cases (Brown v. Board of Education [347 U.S. 483, 1954], Miranda v. Arizona [384 U.S. 436, 1966], and Gideon v. Wainwright [372 U.S. 335, 1963]) to a RIS format file at /home/ga/Documents/landmark_cases.ris. The data source is a real database of 10 historical legal references (6 landmark Supreme Court cases and 3 law review articles) injected into the Jurism citation management software. The task requires selecting only the 3 specified cases while avoiding exporting other cases in the library (Marbury v. Madison, Obergefell v. Hodges, Tinker v. Des Moines). All case citations, dates, and reporter volumes have been verified against authoritative sources including Supreme Court official records.
